content.problemStatement: You are given a positive integer NNN. Your task is to find the last digit of NNN, i.e., the remainder when NNN is divided by 10.

solution.approach:
**Direct Modulo Approach** (when language supports large integers):



Read the number NNN.
Compute Nmod  10N \mod 10Nmod10.
Output the result.



**String-Based Approach** (for numbers larger than native integer types):



Read NNN as a string.
The **last digit** is the last character of the string.
Convert it to an integer if needed and output.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T)O(T)O(T) — constant time per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1)O(1)O(1) for numeric approach, O(L)O(L)O(L) if storing the number as a string of length LLL.
